records.ety: [OE. bordure, F. bordure, fr. border to border, fr. bord a border; of German origin; cf. MHG. borte border, trimming, G. borte trimming, ribbon; akin to E. board in sense 8. See Board, n., and cf. Bordure.]

defs: The outer part or edge of anything, as of a garment, a garden, etc.; margin; verge; brink.
quotes: Upon the borders of these solitudes.

defs: A boundary; a frontier of a state or of the settled part of a country; a frontier district.
A strip or stripe arranged along or near the edge of something, as an ornament or finish.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Bordered (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Bordering.]

defs: To touch at the edge or boundary; to be contiguous or adjacent; -- with on or upon as, Connecticut borders on Massachusetts.
To approach; to come near to; to verge.
quotes: Wit which borders upon profaneness deserves to be branded as folly.

defs: To make a border for; to furnish with a border, as for ornament; as, to border a garment or a garden.
To be, or to have, contiguous to; to touch, or be touched, as by a border; to be, or to have, near the limits or boundary; as, the region borders a forest, or is bordered on the north by a forest.
quotes: The country is bordered by a broad tract called the “hot region.”
Shebah and Raamah . . . border the sea called the Persian gulf.

defs: To confine within bounds; to limit.
quotes: That nature, which contemns its origin, Can not be bordered certain in itself.
